description:
This image contains two line charts, (a) and (b), illustrating phase (φ) change over time, relevant to Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) Persistent Scatterer (PS) techniques. Both charts display a y-axis labelled 'φ' and an x-axis labelled 'time'. Both include a horizontal blue line at φ=0 and a vertical blue line at time=0.

Chart (a) shows phase data represented by black triangles that originate at φ=0 at time=0 and exhibit a linear decrease over time, closely following a red dotted trend line. The total phase decrease for these points is approximately 2π over the displayed time range. Light blue triangles represent a broader distribution of background data points around this trend.

Chart (b) presents an alternative scenario. Black triangles again originate at φ=0 at time=0, but their phase decreases in a non-linear, stepped pattern. A second set of data points, red triangles, start near φ=0 and show a relatively stable or slightly decreasing trend over time, modelled by a red dotted line. The vertical difference between the final phase values of the black triangles and the red triangles is explicitly annotated as 2π, indicating a phase ambiguity or unwrapping jump. The light blue triangles in (b) show a similar broad distribution of background data points as in (a).
